feed.title: SAP Community - SAP AI Launchpad
feed.subtitle: SAP AI Launchpad Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: GenAI HUB SDK missing Model
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We are currently implementing various applications based on the Gen_AI_HUB.
With the latest update of the models in the Generative AI Hub, the following models from Google
have been added:

These models can be created as a configuration and then implemented in SAP AI Launchapd .
After implementation, these are then also available in the Generative AI hub  and can be used as a prompt.

However, the implementation is still missing in the generative-ai-hub-sdk (current version 1.2.2)
I have refactored the library and unfortunately only the following models are currently implemented in the SDK.

 

Deployment.prediction_urls.register({
    'gpt-35-turbo': OPEN_AI_CHAT_COMPLETION,
    'gpt-35-turbo-16k': OPEN_AI_CHAT_COMPLETION,
    'gpt-4': OPEN_AI_CHAT_COMPLETION,
    'gpt-4-32k': OPEN_AI_CHAT_COMPLETION,
    'text-embedding-ada-002': OPEN_AI_EMBEDDING,
    'tiiuae--falcon-40b-instruct': OPENSOURCE_COMPLETION
})

 

Before I implement my own SDK I wanted to ask if the new models will be implemented in the generative-ai-hub-sdk (by SAP) and if so if there is already a release date for the new version.

Greetings
Dom

entry.title: SAP AI Launchpad: Dead process after 2 hours
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have created a basic execution, it ended up in Dead state with below status:

{
"details": [
{
"container_name": "main",
"exit_code": "",
"last_log_messages": "Error pulling container main logs",
"message": "Back-off pulling image ***/house-price:02\"",
"pod_name": "efdb960a3aff0aad-mycodeblock1-3924317824",
"ready": false
},
{
"container_name": "wait",
"exit_code": "",
"last_log_messages": "...-05-02T12:10:***\" level=info msg=\"Alloc=5477 TotalAlloc=13200 Sys=17383 NumGC=58 Goroutines=7\"time=\"2024-05-02T12:15:***\" level=info msg=\"Alloc=5457 TotalAlloc=13303 Sys=17383 NumGC=61 Goroutines=7\"",
"message": "",
"pod_name": "efdb960a3aff0aad-mycodeblock1-3924317824",
"ready": true
}
],
"workflow_info": [
{
"exit_code": "",
"id": "efdb960a3aff0aad",
"message": "child 'efdb960a3aff0aad-3924317824' failed",
"name": "efdb960a3aff0aad",
"outputs": "",
"phase": "Failed",
"type": "Steps"
},
{
"exit_code": "",
"id": "efdb960a3aff0aad-3924317824",
"message": "Step exceeded its deadline",
"name": "efdb960a3aff0aad[0].mypredictor",
"outputs": "",
"phase": "Failed",
"type": "Pod"
},
{
"exit_code": "",
"id": "efdb960a3aff0aad-885417400",
"message": "child 'efdb960a3aff0aad-3924317824' failed",
"name": "efdb960a3aff0aad[0]",
"outputs": "",
"phase": "Failed",
"type": "StepGroup"
}
]
}

Regards,

Niharika.

entry.title: SAP AI Launchpad - Deployment is in pending status for a day with No errors in logs
entry.content: Deployments in SAP AI Launchpad struck in Pending status, same docker image when deployed in local it works fine. Not seeing any errors in logs difficult to debug it.

entry.title: AI Core - Model Training and Inference for Multi output with multi Input types
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I’m working on a complex machine learning project using SAP AI Core, and I’ve hit a roadblock. The goal is to train and deploy a model that handles multiple input types (both numerical and categorical) and generates multiple outputs, which also include numerical and categorical values.

Problem Statement

 

  1. Model Training:
    • I have tried using models such as DecisionTreeRegressor, DecisionTreeClassifier, MultiOutputClassifier, and MultiOutputRegressor.
    • For encoding non-numerical fields, I’ve used LabelEncoder, OneHotEncoder, and MultiLabelBinarizer.
    • The training process sometimes fails, particularly when dealing with complex encoding schemes and multi-output formats.
    • Model Inference:
      • When training is successful, inference often fails. The primary issues are:
        • Difficulty in parsing multi-output results.
        • Challenges in converting outputs back to their original non-numerical forms.
        • Mapping values correctly to their respective output fields.

Detailed Steps and Issues

 

  1. Encoding:
    • For categorical inputs, I’ve tried both LabelEncoder for label encoding and OneHotEncoder for one-hot encoding.
    • For outputs, especially multi-label ones, I’ve used MultiLabelBinarizer.
    • However, combining these encoders with multi-output models seems to introduce complexity, particularly in maintaining the correct mappings during inference.
  2. Model Training:
    • Example code:

from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeRegressor

from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier

from sklearn.model_selection import KFold, train_test_split

from sklearn.inspection import permutation_importance

from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder

from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder

from sklearn.preprocessing import MultiLabelBinarizer

from sklearn.multioutput import MultiOutputClassifier

from sklearn.multioutput import MultiOutputRegressor

from sklearn.compose import make_column_transformer

# Assume X_train and y_train are preprocessed input and output datasets model =

clf = MultiOutputClassifier(DecisionTreeClassifier(max_depth=DT_MAX_DEPTH, random_state=31))  

    clf.fit(train_x, Y_encoded)

  • Issues:
  • Encoding-decoding mismatches.
  • Models not handling mixed input-output types effectively.

 

  1. Inference:
    • Example code:

query = dict(call_request.json)

    input_features = [ # list of values from request call

        query['Company_code'],

        query['Vendor'],

        query['Amount'],

        query['InvoiceDocumenttype'],

        query['Fiscalyear'],

    ]

 

# Convert to DataFrame to match the input structure of the encoder  

    input_df = pd.DataFrame([input_features], columns=['Company_code', 'Vendor', 'Amount', 'InvoiceDocumenttype', 'Fiscalyear'])  

 

    # Preprocess input_features using one-hot encoder  

    input_features_encoded = encoder_X.transform(input_df)

 

    # Make prediction  

    prediction = model.predict(input_features_encoded)  

 

    # Postprocess prediction to original labels  

    prediction = encoder_Y.inverse_transform(prediction)

    # Map the prediction to the field names

    fields = ['HKONT','KOSTL', 'Profitcenters', 'Paymentterms', 'Partnerbanktype', 'Taxcode']

    prediction_dict = dict(zip(fields, prediction[0]))

  • Issues:
    • Decoding predictions back to original categorical values.
    • Ensuring the predictions map correctly to their respective fields.

Questions

  • Has anyone successfully implemented a multi-input multi-output model that handles both numerical and categorical data?
  • Are there specific strategies or best practices for encoding and decoding in such scenarios?
  • Can you recommend any resources or examples that deal with similar complexities?

Any insights, suggestions, or references would be immensely helpful!

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

entry.title: AI Launchpad: "Generative AI Hub" menu not displayed
entry.content:
Hi community,

I've been exploring the discovery center mission: Set Up SAP AI Core Generative AI Hub. We have subscriptions to SAP AI Core (extended plan) and SAP AI Launchpad (free plan).

I'm on the step "Configure AI Launchpad".

As per the task description, "Generatie AI Hub" menu should be visible.

However, in our environment, I don't see the "Generatie AI Hub" menu.

I've read the prerequisits in the document and assigned "ailaunchpad_genai_manager" to my user. After the assignment, I re-logged on to the service.

I would like to confirm if "Generatie AI Hub" menu is only available for standard plan, or I am missing any configurations.

entry.title: About Retrieval Augmented Generation using generative-ai-hub-sdk and HANA vector search Tutorial
entry.content:
Dear BTP Experts,
I tried SAP Generative AI with this tutorial, but when I execute Step 7's code, I had encountered the Value Error blow. So, I'd try to change another API model according to this document but it didn't work.

Does anyone know to solve this?
Best regards,
Yuta Takahashi

entry.title: using Python - Is there any reference GitHub repo to Develop a multitenant Software as a Service app
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are planning to create a multitenant SaaS app using Python. By referring to the example, we have built a solution for a single tenant—a Python micro-service app to consume foundation models through the Generative AI Hub (https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-generative-ai-hub-use-cases/tree/main/01-social-media-citizen-reporting-genai-hub/python_app). This solution works fine within the same tenant.

Now, we need to make the Python application a multitenant SaaS application. Is there any GitHub reference or documentation available for this purpose?

I have checked the SAP Discovery Center, but the multitenant applications are mainly for CAP, and I couldn't find any references for Python applications.

Any suggestions or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you,
Sabari

entry.title: Deployment for python project using generative ai foundational models in ai launchpad
entry.content:
I have created a ipynb file implementing RAG with HANA. For local development I am using service keys from Ai core after setting the same as environment variables on my machine. 

Now, how do i deploy the same to BTP?

Do i deploy as cf application? if yes, is there any reference on how to provide ai core as a service dependency?

Do i deploy in ai core using workflows? if yes, is there any reference on how to provide ai core as a service dependency?

Thanks

Kayur

SAP AI Launchpad SAP AI Core @carlosbasto

entry.title: Viewing customer failed in Support Launchpad
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

for a customer of my organization, we are unable to view it in the list of customers in order to open an incident or request a license.

Viewing customer failed in Support Launchpad, but in the list of On-Premise Active Sales Contracts result active

All our users are enabled and have permissions to access for these features.

Has anyone ever had this kind of issue?

thanks to anyone who can help me.

entry.title: Is Single Tenancy for LLMs Available in SAP GenAI Hub or Planned for Future Releases?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
 
I’m exploring options for integrating Large Language Models (LLMs) within our SAP landscape, specifically through the SAP GenAI Hub. One of our key requirements is ensuring single tenancy, where the LLMs are dedicated exclusively to our organization, and no infrastructure or data is shared with other clients.
 
I haven’t come across explicit details on whether single tenancy is a feature currently offered by the SAP GenAI Hub for LLMs, or if this is something SAP plans to support in future releases.
 
Can anyone clarify:
 
1. Is single tenancy for LLMs available on the SAP GenAI Hub?
2. If not, are there any plans in SAP’s roadmap to introduce this feature, particularly for customers with strict data privacy and isolation requirements?
3. Are there recommended alternative approaches to achieve single-tenancy or similar levels of isolation if this feature is not currently available?
 
Thanks in advance for your insights!

entry.title: CAP LLM Plugin vs GenAI javascript SDK + document grounding
entry.content: With the new GenAI Javascript SDK and the document grounding step in orchestration, I guess that CAP LLM Plugin will be discontinued. is there anyone who can confirm ?

entry.title: Business Context For Foundation Models In AI Launchpad
entry.content:
Hi 

I am trying to consume Foundation models from SAP Generative AI hub. As part of this i have created Configuration and Deployment url in SAP AI Launchpad.

I am able to access the deployment url from post man.

 

It was answering my questions , Suppose if i ask captial of England ,it gaves me London .

Now I want to use this in one of SAP based application.The answers must be based on SAP Application data.

Suppose if i ask what is name of customer no 101 ,Then it should be from SAP Business Partner Details.

Right now it was not giving information specific to SAP.

How is it linked to SAP specif data .

Can some one elobrate steps to achive this

entry.title: SAP AI Launchpad application url connection is not private
entry.content:
Dear community, 

I faced below webpage error when launching SAP AI Launchpad application free-tier edition. It only happened today 29-Nov-2024. Any idea how to resolve this?

#AILaunchpad #SAPAILaunchpad

Regards,

Ben

entry.title: How to use saved prompts in sap ai launchpad
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have configured and deployed a foundation model in SAP AI Launchpad, and we can use it programmatically now. There is a save prompts option in SAP generative ai hub; how can I access these saved model prompts programmatically in the python code running in bas and deployed in cloud foundary?


We are currently using prompts in the code, and it would be extremely helpful if we could use those saved prompts from the launchpad.

Thanks.

entry.title: Can we use SAP AI core to deploy the application through Docker Image and use the application from E
entry.content:
I have followed some of Tutorials of SAP AI core. But I am not getting the correct way to deploy the Application using Docker Image and use it later on via an Endpoint. 

I have Chatbot appliation. I have created the Docker compose file because it has two containers running on sideto make the application working. I have no idea how to deploy the application to SAP AI core and use an application via its endpoint.

Could anyone explain me this ?

entry.title: SAP A.I. Privacy and Security
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

An important factor with generative A.I. is security - Does our data we submit to an A.I. train a model and potentially thereby expose confidential information?

AWS Bedrock is an A.I. service offered by Amazon and they clearly state that it is secure and private. It is safe to submit confidential information.

SAP A.I. Launchpad / Generative A.I. Hub offers access to many models - are these models hosted on SAP servers or does it just connect to the A.I. providers servers?

We're looking for a definitive answer that submitting confidential/sensitive data to a model on SAP A.I. Launchpad is safe, and that our data is not exposed to the public internet or used to train models?

Kind Regards,

Reinhardt

entry.title: Ai launchpad What can be substitute for installing docker desktop in connecting with AI core
entry.content:
Hello team , I am unable to install docker desktop application.please can you share an alternative.

I am able to connect GitHub successfully and executed a pipline but stuck at docker step . Due to system rights limitation I won't be able to install docker desktop app ..can you share alternatives .. thank you

entry.title: SAP AI Launchpad and GitHub
entry.content: I'm working with GitHub and SAP AI Launchpad, and my connection between GitHub and SAP AI Launchpad tends to disconnect, in the application part it doesn't sync and I have already verified that the link to my repository is correct. What can I do?

entry.title: Predictive ai with sap ai core
entry.content: Has anyone done the learning predictive ai with sap ai core tutorial on ingesting live data into your house price predictor with sap ai core, someone can help me because I'm stuck at step 15?

entry.title: Licensing question for Generative AI Hub API access: AI Core vs AI Launchpad
entry.content:
SAP AI Core SAP AI Launchpad #Generative AI Hub

I am considering developing a Generative AI application using SAP Generative AI Hub.

I am currently researching licensing information and have found that SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad are required.

I am envisioning a use case where a Generative AI application developed with Generative AI Hub is called as an API from an external application. In this case, is SAP AI Launchpad also mandatory?

I have also seen some descriptions in Community Blogs stating that AI Core alone is sufficient, which is causing confusion. (I have lost the URLs of the blogs.)

Could you please advise on the licensing requirements when exposing the application externally as an API?
